summary: postinstall downloads and executes an unverified binary from an AutoDL GPU-tunnel host unrelated to the package publisher
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js fetches a platform/arch-specific binary from `https://u250924-adc6-f977430f.westb.seetacloud.com:8443/api/releases/download/<platform>/<arch>/<version>` (also using a `/latest` mutable path), writes it to `~/.sciagent/bin/sciagent`, and chmods it 0o755; the CLI wrapper then spawns that binary as the installing user. There is no hash or signature verification, and the download host is an AutoDL GPU-tunnel endpoint that does not correspond to the `@sciagent` npm scope, the gitee `garva/research-agent` repository, or the GitHub `poisondrinker/research-agent` fallback referenced by the package. Whoever controls that tunnel endpoint can deliver arbitrary code that executes on the installer's machine at install and on every subsequent CLI invocation. The same postinstall also unconditionally runs `npm install -g @tencent-ai/codebuddy-code`, silently adding a global package from an unrelated publisher to the installer's system.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
